license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6cyhaxw
title: Biohazard Garbage Protocol: Autoclave Biohazard Waste
author: Steven W Wilhelm

steps_raw.instruction: If the waste container is > ¾ full, then it needs to be processed for autoclaving.
Autoclave the processed waste on gravity cycle.
Instructions on cycle times based on the amount of trash you are autoclaving are on the autoclave.
Transfer bag into a wide autoclavable tray. Tray will have PP on bottom. Don't use HDPE! It will MELT !
Pour 100 mL di water into bag containing trash.
Pour 100 mL di water into tray. Between the container and the bag.
Close bag loosely put a strip of autoclave tape on bag.
After autoclaved waste has cooled, put the autoclaved waste into designated trash bin next to autoclave.
Transfer sharps container into a wide autoclavable tray. Tray will have PP on bottom. Don't use HDPE! It will MELT!
Pour 100 mL water into interior of the sharps container.
Close lid until it snaps shut and put autoclave tape on lid.
After autoclaved waste has cooled, put the autoclaved waste into designated trash bin next to autoclave.
